kansas_city_april_2026-04-03_2026-04-06_.packing:
**Packing List for Kansas City (April 3-6, 2026)**

**👕 Clothing**
- 3 tops (lightweight)
- 2 bottoms (jeans or pants)
- 1 dress or jumpsuit (optional)
- 1 light jacket or sweater
- Undergarments and socks
- Comfortable walking shoes
- Sandals or flip-flops (if weather permits)
- Umbrella or raincoat (in case of rain)

**💊 Health**
- Asthma medication (inhalers and/or pills)
- Spacer device (if using)
- Peak flow meter
- Prescription medications and copies of prescriptions
- EpiPen (if prescribed)
- Mask or respirator (for dust or pollen protection)
- Hand sanitizer

**🧴 Essentials**
- Travel documents (passport, ID, etc.)
- Phone and charger
- Wallet and credit cards
- Snacks (e.g., granola bars, nuts)
- Water bottle
- Travel-sized essentials (toothbrush, toothpaste, etc.)
- Adapters and converters (if necessary)
kansas_city_april_2026-04-03_2026-04-06_.itinerary:
**Day 1: Arrival and Exploring Downtown Kansas City 🌆**

- 9:00 AM: Arrive at Kansas City International Airport (MCI)
- 10:00 AM: Take a Uber or Lyft to the hotel (around 30 minutes)
- 11:00 AM: Check-in at the hotel and freshen up
- 12:00 PM: Lunch at Arthur Bryant's, a Kansas City BBQ institution 🍖
- 1:30 PM: Visit the National World War I Museum and Memorial 🏛️
- 4:00 PM: Explore the vibrant Crossroads Arts District, known for its street art and galleries
- 7:00 PM: Dinner at The Golden Ox, a historic steakhouse 🍖
- 9:00 PM: End the day with live music at the Green Lady Lounge 🎶

**Day 2: BBQ and Fountains 🌸**

- 9:00 AM: Breakfast at Eggtc., a local breakfast spot 🍳
- 10:30 AM: Visit the iconic Kauffman Memorial Garden and Fountain 🌺
- 12:30 PM: Lunch at Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que, another BBQ favorite 🍖
- 2:00 PM: Explore the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, featuring an impressive art collection 🎨
- 5:00 PM: Visit the stunning Country Club Plaza, an outdoor shopping and dining district 🏙️
- 7:30 PM: Dinner at Lidia's Kansas City, an Italian restaurant with a Kansas City twist 🍝

**Day 3: History and Jazz 🎶**

- 9:00 AM: Breakfast at the American Restaurant, located in the Westin Crown Center 🍳
- 10:30 AM: Visit the National Museum of Toys and Miniatures, featuring an incredible collection of toys and miniatures 🎀
- 12:30 PM: Lunch at the iconic Arthur Bryant's, again! 🍖
- 2:00 PM: Explore the American Jazz Museum, celebrating the rich jazz heritage of Kansas City 🎺
- 5:00 PM: Visit the vibrant 18th & Vine neighborhood, home to the Negro Leagues Baseball Museum and the American Jazz Museum
- 7:30 PM: Dinner at the Stock Hill, an upscale steakhouse

chicago_april_2026-04-04_2026-04-07_flig.itinerary:
**Chicago 4-Day Itinerary (2026-04-04 to 2026-04-07)**

**Day 1: Arrival in Chicago 🛬**

- **Morning (9:00 AM - 10:00 AM)**: Arrive at **O'Hare International Airport (ORD)**. Take a **taxi** or **Uber** from the airport to your hotel. The journey should take around 30-45 minutes, depending on traffic.
- **10:00 AM - 12:00 PM**: Check-in to your hotel and freshen up. Visit **Millennium Park** to explore iconic landmarks like the **Cloud Gate** (The Bean) and take a stroll along the **Lake Michigan shoreline**.
- **12:00 PM - 1:00 PM**: Grab lunch at **Xoco**, a casual Mexican spot from Rick Bayless, known for its delicious street food-style dishes.
- **1:00 PM - 5:00 PM**: Visit the **Art Institute of Chicago**, one of the largest and most visited art museums in the country.
- **Evening (6:00 PM - 8:00 PM)**: Experience the city's vibrant nightlife by taking a stroll along the **Riverwalk** and enjoying dinner at a local restaurant.

**Day 2: Explore Chicago's Iconic Spots 🌆**

- **Morning (9:00 AM - 10:00 AM)**: Start the day with breakfast at **Wildberry Cafe**, a local favorite serving creative breakfast dishes.
- **10:00 AM - 12:00 PM**: Take a short walk to the **Willis Tower (formerly Sears Tower)** and enjoy panoramic views of the city from the Skydeck on the 103rd floor.
- **12:00 PM - 1:00 PM**: Grab lunch at **Portillo's**, a Chicago institution famous for its hot dogs, Italian beef sandwiches, and chocolate cake.
- **1:00 PM - 4:00 PM**: Visit **Navy Pier**, a lakefront attraction offering boat cruises, an amusement park, and entertainment options.
- **Evening (6:00 PM - 8:00 PM)**: Enjoy dinner at **Gibsons Italia**, a historic Italian steakhouse.

**Day 3: Chicago's Neighborhoods and Culture 🌈**

- **Morning (9:00 AM - 10:00 AM)**:

new_york_april_2026-04-04_2026-04-07_fli.itinerary:

- Arrive at JFK/LGA/EWR airport, take a taxi or subway to your hotel
- Visit Times Square 🌆 and try a classic New York-style pizza 🍕
- Explore the nearby Broadway area and catch a musical 🎭

- Start the day at the Statue of Liberty 🗽️ and Ellis Island
- Walk across the Brooklyn Bridge 🌉 for spectacular city views
- Try a NYC-style bagel 🥯 with lox and cream cheese for breakfast

🌞 Day 3: 2026-04-06 - Museums and Parks 🎨

- Visit Central Park 🌳 for a stroll or rent a bike

- Take a walk along the High Line 🚶‍♀️ for city views

colorado_april_2026-04-04_2026-04-07_car.itinerary:
**Day 1: Denver to Estes Park (April 4, 2026)** 🌅

- 8:00 AM: Depart from Denver, start driving on I-27 towards Estes Park (approx. 1-hour drive)
- 9:00 AM: Stop at the **Rocky Mountain National Park Visitor Center** ( RMNP) for information and park passes
- 10:00 AM: Explore the **Bear Lake Trail**, a scenic hiking spot with breathtaking views
- 12:30 PM: Lunch at **The Grille at The Historic Stanley Hotel**, try their famous Elk burgers
- 2:00 PM: Drive through the scenic **Trail Ridge Road**, the highest paved road in the US (approx. 1 hour)
- 5:00 PM: Arrive at Estes Park, check-in at your hotel
- 7:30 PM: Dinner at **Smokin' Dave's BBQ**, enjoy their slow-cooked BBQ
- Weather: Partly cloudy, 50°F (10°C)

**Day 2: Estes Park to Boulder (April 5, 2026)** 🌞

- 8:00 AM: Depart from Estes Park, drive on US-34 towards Boulder (approx. 1.5 hours)
- 10:00 AM: Stop at the **Chautauqua Trail**, a scenic hiking spot with great views of Boulder
- 12:00 PM: Lunch at **The Med**, try their Mediterranean-inspired dishes
- 1:30 PM: Visit the **Pearl Street Mall**, a vibrant shopping and dining district
- 3:30 PM: Explore the **University of Colorado Museum of Natural History**, featuring exhibits on Colorado's natural history
- 6:00 PM: Dinner at **The Kitchen**, enjoy their farm-to-table cuisine
- Weather: Sunny, 62°F (17°C)

**Day 3: Boulder to Aspen (April 6, 2026)** 🏔️

- 8:00 AM: Depart from Boulder, drive on CO-93 towards Aspen (approx. 4 hours)
- 12:00 PM: Stop at the **Maroon Bells**, one of the most photographed mountains in Colorado
- 1:30 PM: Lunch at **The Little Nell**, try their upscale American cuisine
- 3:00 PM: Explore the **Aspen Saturday

colorado_april_2026-04-04_2026-04-07_fli.itinerary:
🌞 Day 1: 2026-04-04 - Arrival in Colorado 🛫️
Arrive at Denver International Airport, take the rail line to Union Station 🚂. Check-in to hotel, explore LoDo (Lower Downtown) neighborhood, and try local food like Rocky Mountain Oysters 🍴. 

🏔️ Day 2: 2026-04-05 - Rocky Mountain National Park 🏞️
Drive to Rocky Mountain National Park (2 hours) and hike the Bear Lake Trail 🏃‍♀️. Enjoy the scenic views and spot local wildlife 🦌. Try some bison burgers for dinner 🍔.

🌄 Day 3: 2026-04-06 - Boulder and Denver 🌆
Visit the Pearl Street Mall in Boulder and explore the city's vibrant atmosphere 🎨. Return to Denver and visit the Denver Art Museum 🎭. Try some green chili at a local restaurant 🌶.

❄️ Day 4: 2026-04-07 - Skiing or Snowboarding 🏂
Spend the day skiing or snowboarding at Winter Park Resort (2 hours away) or try the downtown Denver ski rentals 🏂. Alternatively, visit the Denver Botanic Gardens if weather doesn't permit skiing 🌺. Depart from Denver International Airport 🛫️.
